channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Trump says Iran agreed to not have nuclear weapons, but 'they can change their mind'
item.description: The president was speaking to the New York Post's "Pod Force One" podcast in a wide-ranging interview.

item.title: Macy's posts strongest Q1 growth in four years, raises guidance despite consumer worries
item.description: Macy's has been in the midst of a turnaround under CEO Tony Spring, who has worked to close underperforming stores and reinvest in ones worth keeping open.

item.title: Mortgage rates are easing slightly, but homebuyers are retreating
item.description: Mortgage rates fell slightly last week, but neither current homeowners nor potential homebuyers were inspired. Demand for mortgages fell overall.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: Blackstone, Marvell Technology, Palo Alto Networks & more
item.description: These are the stocks posting the largest moves premarket.

item.title: Stock futures are little changed after S&P 500 closes at another record: Live updates
item.description: The three major indexes rose to fresh record highs on Tuesday.

item.title: This income-generating asset offers yield exceeding 6% at a tax-advantaged rate
item.description: Investors who can stomach some rate volatility may be poised to pick up solid yield.

item.title: Eli Lilly's top dealmaker says don't be surprised to see more M&A that pushes Lilly into new areas
item.description: Eli Lilly's new top dealmaker explains how the company changed its approach to M&A now that it's the most valuable pharmaceutical company in the world.

item.title: France's Macron invites Sam Altman to attend G7, OpenAI tells CNBC
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has looked to win over tech bosses amid hopes to boost AI capability.

item.title: U.S. proposes fresh tariffs on 60 economies over forced labor trade practices
item.description: USTR has proposed a 10% duty rate for economies that have adopted a full or partial prohibition on forced labor trade, and 12.5% for all other economies.

item.title: Fed Chair Warsh makes first hires at central bank, including ‘Project 2025’ author
item.description: The new Fed chair has made his first hires. One wrote the Fed chapter in conservative policy blueprint "Project 2025."

item.title: Hilton, Becerra lead primary race for California governor; Bass ahead in LA mayoral race
item.description: Republican television commentator Steve Hilton and Democratic former cabinet secretary Xavier Becerra took the early lead in the open primary race for governor.

item.title: Josh Turek wins Iowa Democratic Senate primary that could shape Democrats' path to Senate majority
item.description: Iowa's Senate primary between state Rep. Josh Turek and state Sen. Zach Wahls could be critical as Democrats look to take back power in Congress.

item.title: Palo Alto Networks tops earnings as AI fuels cybersecurity urgency
item.description: The beat comes on lowered expectations, after the company gave disappointing guidance in February that fell short of analyst estimates.

item.title: SpaceX is worth less than half of its $1.75 trillion IPO target, Morningstar says
item.description: The analysts see a wide range of possibilities around xAI and find its "economic moat indeterminate."

item.title: Pulte appointment as spy chief would give a Trump attack dog access to a trove of intelligence
item.description: In his role atop the housing finance agency, Bill Pulte has used his access to mortgage records to refer some top Trump opponents for prosecution.

item.title: KKR, Ares, Blackstone tumble premarket as Partners Group caps private equity fund withdrawals
item.description: U.S. private markets firms fell in premarket trading, as concerns over redemption pressure returned to the spotlight.

item.title: Perplexity CEO tells CNBC one metric will determine who wins the AI race
item.description: Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas said that whichever company can provide the "most taken value per watt per user" will be the AI winners in the future.

item.title: Former Barclays CEO Bob Diamond says Middle East is primed for tokenized finance
item.description: Middle Eastern banks are at an advantage as they are more agile with less worries about legacy systems, according to Diamond.

item.title: Trump still protected from tax enforcement, but anti-weaponization fund is dead, Blanche says
item.description: The DOJ's $1.8 billion fund faced strong criticism because it could have compensated people convicted of crimes related to the Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol Riot.

item.title: CBS fires veteran correspondent Scott Pelley amid turmoil over direction of '60 Minutes'
item.description: CBS News has fired high-profile "60 Minutes" correspondent Scott Pelley amid debate about the direction of the show, a mainstay of the network.

item.title: We're upping our Palo Alto price target after strong earnings vanquish AI disruption fears
item.description: The business momentum is clear here, validating the stock's dramatic comeback to fresh highs.

item.title: Ending soon: Earn 150,000 miles with this all-time-high Capital One Venture Business offer
item.description: It's rare to see offers this large attached to credit cards with sub-$100 annual fees, like the Capital One Venture Business.

item.title: Ulta shares pop as beauty retailer beats Wall Street expectations and hikes earnings outlook
item.description: Ulta Beauty beat on the top and bottom lines for its fiscal first quarter on Tuesday as CEO Kecia Steelman praised the company's strategy.

item.title: UK assets face underestimated risk event, analysts warn as PM hopeful swerves call to calm investor nerves
item.description: One analyst said markets would reprice U.K. fiscal risk "accordingly and quickly" if Burnham's political ambitions come to light.

item.title: OECD warns of global slowdown as U.S.-Iran war stymies economic growth prospects
item.description: The OECD has cut its global growth outlook, warning of lasting damage caused by the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Cassidy: Pulte not 'competent' for intelligence director role
item.description: Intelligence community veterans have warned that Pulte is ill-prepared for the job and could target Trump's foes in the role.

item.title: Supreme Court allows Alabama to use congressional map that dilutes Black vote
item.description: The ruling is a victory for Republicans, who have sought to retain their slim majority in the House of Representatives by redistricting in various states.

item.title: Investors bet humanoid robots will transform industry and homes over the next decade
item.description: “Be ready for it,” said one fund manager who sees enormous opportunities in the space.

item.title: Novo Nordisk rolls out Wegovy pill in the UAE — testing appetite outside the U.S. with first global launch
item.description: Novo is "all in" on launching the Wegovy pill in overseas markets, and it sees it as a "major opportunity."

item.title: Alphabet's $80 billion stock sale leaves Wall Street in 'unprecedented territory,' says Goldman's Gutman
item.description: Goldman Sachs is acting as a joint book-running manager for Alphabet's underwritten offerings.
